title: Siege / Hostile-Takeover Contest State
description: Authoritative record of ONE org-vs-org hostile-takeover contest against a territory claim (or a non-citizen-secured player-city node). This is the finalized DELIBERATE-TEMPO state machine for the MUSH's planned-but-undelivered 'Drop 6D' (Guide_11 §7): a multi-phase contest (declared -> mustering -> assault(1..N) -> resolution -> cooldown) decided by a server-owned CONTROL METER [0-100] with a HIGH capture threshold held for a duration. Rare, planned, telegraphed. Server owns it and ALL timers/scoring; resolution uses NO RNG; it survives a server restart (world_state.dat / sieges table) and resumes via a deterministic catch-up. See docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md for the full state diagram, capture math, defaults, and rules. Supersedes the single-window attacker_fraction sketch in FACTION_TERRITORY_DESIGN §6.

source.source_policy: Original design for Drop 6D, which the MUSH specced as 'Planned' but never delivered. Grounded in Guide_11's claim/influence model and the security-zone consent model; recast Clone Wars era and adapted to a server-authoritative Godot server. New mechanic => docs/DIVERGENCE_LEDGER.md row (reserve DIV-0020; implementer verifies next-free) BEFORE implementation. Numbers are deliberate-tempo starting points, owner-tunable, NOT flagged owner rulings.

claim_id.description: The territory_claim under contest. (For a city node, the city's claim id.)

node_id.description: The contested node. Must be a live claim or a non-citizen-secured city expansion node.

zone_id.description: Zone the node belongs to. Base security must be contested or lawless. Sieges cannot be declared on secured zones or on city-upgraded-to-secured (citizen-secured) rooms (the citadel rule).

defender_org_id.description: Current owning org (the claim's org_id at declaration).

attacker_org_id.description: Challenging org. Must meet config.attacker_min_influence in the zone (default 40 / Dominant tier per territory_model.gd), config.rank_to_declare (default 4), and config.attacker_min_treasury to declare.

declared_by_char_id.description: Attacker-org character who declared (rank-gated; default rank 4+).

state.description: Current state-machine state. Deliberate-tempo spine: declared -> mustering -> assault(1..N) -> resolution -> cooldown, with an inter-assault 'lull'. Terminal archive states: captured, repelled, aborted. 'assault' is entered N times (config.assault_count), tracked by schedule.current_assault_index. Transitions and dwell-times are defined in docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md §3. Advanced ONLY by the server slow tick / rank-gated intents; never by a client.

phase_started_unix.description: Wall-clock epoch seconds the current state was entered. Timer math is relative to this.

phase_deadline_unix.description: Epoch seconds at which the current state auto-advances (e.g. grace/mustering/lull/cooldown end) or the current assault window ends. For assault states this equals the current schedule.assault_windows[current_assault_index].end_unix.

declared_unix.description: Epoch seconds the siege was declared.

war_chest_credits.description: Credits escrowed from the attacker treasury at declaration (config.declare_cost_credits). Refunded per config.withdraw_refund_fraction_grace on an in-grace withdrawal; forfeit once the siege presses past the grace window.

config.description: Snapshot of the timing/threshold/gate config this siege runs under, captured at declaration so a live siege is immune to an admin retune. ALL values owner-tunable; defaults shown are DELIBERATE-TEMPO starting points, NOT a flagged owner decision baked in. See docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md §8 for the master defaults table and a compressed prototype/dev profile.

declaration_grace_hours.description: DECLARED dwell: cheap-withdraw grace + first defender notification before the mustering clock starts.

mustering_hours.description: MUSTERING dwell: defender warning/prep window before assault #1; the only window in which allies may commit. No forced PvP yet.

assault_count.description: Number of scheduled assault windows (the N in assault(1..N)).

assault_window_hours.description: ASSAULT dwell: duration of each no-consent PvP + control-capture window.

lull_hours.description: LULL dwell: inter-assault regroup between windows; PvP off; control bleeds toward the defender.

cooldown_hours.description: COOLDOWN dwell: node lockout after a resolved siege before it can be contested again (default 7 days).

abort_cooldown_hours.description: Shorter node lockout after an aborted declaration (anti spam-declare).

capture_threshold.description: Control the attacker must hold to capture (early or at final tally). HIGH by design (> 50): the attacker must clearly dominate; the defender holds on anything short of decisive control.

capture_hold_seconds.description: Continuous seconds control must stay >= capture_threshold DURING an assault window to trigger an EARLY capture (short-circuit to resolution).

control_bleed_per_hour.description: Rate the control meter decays toward control_min OUTSIDE assault windows (mustering/lull/cooldown). Makes a lead perishable; the attacker must press every window.

control_per_pvp_kill.description: Control swing per resolved PvP kill (killer's side; attacker up, defender down). Depends on the owner-gated PvP unlock (DIV-0019).

control_per_guard_defeated.description: Attacker control gain for defeating the defender's stationed guard NPC (PvE; works without PvP).

control_per_hold_tick.description: Per-slow-tick control swing toward whichever side has strictly more living members in scope during an assault (a tie does nothing).

control_per_objective.description: Control swing for an optional node objective (raise a banner / hold a console); either side.

control_per_sabotage.description: Attacker control gain for an optional sabotage action against the node's defenses.

attacker_min_influence.description: Attacker's required org influence in the zone to declare (Dominant tier per territory_model.gd DOMINANT_AT=40). Supersedes the old stub default of 50, which came from the FACTION_TERRITORY_DESIGN 0-150 scale; anchor to the live 0-100 code tiers (claim 20 / dominant 40 / control 70). See docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md §1.

rank_to_declare.description: Attacker org rank to declare / withdraw (above the rank-3 claim gate, below the rank-5 city gate).

declare_cost_credits.description: War-chest escrowed from the attacker treasury at declaration.

attacker_min_treasury.description: Attacker treasury must hold at least this to declare (= declare_cost_credits).

withdraw_refund_fraction_grace.description: Fraction of the war-chest refunded if the attacker withdraws during the DECLARED grace window (0.0 after grace).

max_active_sieges_per_node.description: Concurrent sieges allowed on one node.

max_concurrent_attacks_per_org.description: Outgoing (attacking) sieges an org may run at once. Defenses are unbounded.

intervention_mode.description: Third-party intervention policy. mustering_only (default): allies must commit during the mustering window so the combatant set is locked before assaults. none: strictly the two principals. open: allies may commit before the final assault (higher tempo; not recommended for deliberate tempo).

max_allies_per_side.description: Third-party orgs allowed to commit per side.

ally_min_influence.description: Intervening org's required influence in the zone to commit (claim-floor presence).

ally_rank_to_commit.description: Intervening org's leadership rank to commit an alliance.

schedule.description: The fixed assault schedule computed at the declared->mustering transition so BOTH orgs know exactly when the fights are (the 'planned/telegraphed' core). Assault windows are placed at config offsets from mustering end (docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md §3.3). Persisted; on restart, windows whose start passed during downtime are handled by the deterministic catch-up rule.

current_assault_index.description: 0-based index of the current/next assault window (the i in assault(1..N)).

assault_windows.description: The config.assault_count scheduled windows, in order.

control_meter.description: The single scalar that decides the siege (server-owned; NO RNG). value in [config.control_min, config.control_max]. Moves ONLY from resolved scoring events + control-hold ticks during assault windows, and bleeds toward control_min outside them. Capture: value >= config.capture_threshold, either held for config.capture_hold_seconds during an assault (early capture) or read at the final resolution tally. See docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md §4.

updated_unix.description: Wall-clock of the last meter update (drives bleed math on the next slow tick).

hold_since_unix.description: Epoch the meter first reached capture_threshold in the CURRENT assault; null when below threshold. Early capture fires when now - hold_since_unix >= capture_hold_seconds. Cleared the instant value drops below threshold or an assault window ends.

peak.description: Highest value reached this siege (audit / news color).

pvp_consent.description: TRANSIENT. While state == assault, members of attacker_org, defender_org, and any committed allies are mutually flagged for NO-CONSENT PvP at scope_node_ids, regardless of the zone's normal consent rules. This is the ONLY forced-PvP mechanism; it is tightly scoped (the contesting orgs, the contested node + adjacency, the assault window only). NOT persisted: the server RE-DERIVES active from state each slow tick (true iff state == assault) and never trusts a client. Depends on the owner-gated PvP unlock (DIV-0019).

scope_node_ids.description: Nodes where the no-consent window applies (the claim node plus declared adjacency).

intervenors.description: Third-party orgs that committed to a side (config.intervention_mode; default mustering_only, cap config.max_allies_per_side per side). Allies gain the pvp_consent flag and score for their side but cannot declare/withdraw/concede/negotiate, and do not receive the claim on capture (spoils-sharing is an RP/economy hook, not modeled here). See docs/SIEGE_DESIGN.md §6.

score.description: Cumulative audit totals + a replayable log of every scoring event. Distinct from control_meter: points are UNCLAMPED, never bled (they drive the 'who fought' record and any future CP reward, which is an OPEN OWNER DECISION - no rate baked in). The server owns all of this and consumes RESOLVED action-window outcomes, never raw client input.

contributions.description: Audit log of scoring events that moved the contest. Each row records the raw audit points AND the control_delta it applied to the meter, for a fully replayable history and the post-siege news feed ('did you see what happened').

char_id.description: Contributing character (present for CP-reward attribution; absent for non-character events like a pure hold tick).

assault_index.description: Which assault window this occurred in.

control_delta.description: Signed amount this event applied to control_meter.value (attacker positive, defender negative), after clamping context.

outcome.description: Set at resolution (and finalized at cooldown->archive). captured => claim ownership transfers to attacker_org (guard dismissed, income band re-evaluated), node enters cooldown lockout. repelled => defender keeps the claim, node enters cooldown lockout. aborted => declaration cancelled before/at withdrawal; node gets abort_cooldown_hours lockout. Every terminal outcome emits a Director news headline.

final_control.description: Control-meter value at the tally instant (or at early capture). Replaces the old attacker_fraction: capture iff final_control >= config.capture_threshold.

early_capture.description: True if capture fired via the hold-for-duration short-circuit rather than the final tally.

war_chest_disposition.description: What happened to the escrowed war-chest on resolution/abort.

news_headline.description: Player-facing news line (feeds the Director news bulletin / director_log).

lockout_until_unix.description: Epoch until which the node cannot be re-contested (cooldown_hours after a resolved siege, or abort_cooldown_hours after an abort).

outcome.news_headline: Black Sun storms the port fringe, wresting Dock 9 from the Falleen Syndicate.
